song.title: Having Seen the World’s Fair Beauty
song.author: This song was written shortly after its author, who had been born blind, regained her sight.
song.presentation: V1 C V2 C V3 C V4 C V5 C
song.lyrics: [V1] .G Bm C D G Having seen the world’s fair beauty, . Bm C D Lord, I would be blind once more, .Em Bm Lest I lose the loving Presence .Em C D And Delight I knew before [C] .G D Having seen the world’s fair beauty, .G D Lord, I would be blind once more, .G D Lest I lose the loving Presence .Em C D And Delight I knew before [V2] Dawn and sunset, star and moonlight, I can see in paradise, But while here on earth His mercy For my blindness will suffice. [V3] I no more would be delivered From my thorn, but look for grace, While within the veil His glory Shineth brightly on my face. [V4] In the deep dark night, His beauty I by faith, not sight, may trace; He was ridiculed and hated, Yet I see His glorious face. [V5] Sun and moonlight far surpassing, Clouds and Milky Way o’erspread, Is the glory all transcending Shining from his thorn crowned head
